proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with idh1_mutation=1 have a higher objective_response rate than patients with idh1_mutation=0.
Patients with npm1_mutation=1 have a higher objective_response rate than patients with npm1_mutation=0 (NPM1 is a favorable AML marker).
Patients with tp53_mutation=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with tp53_mutation=0 (TP53 is an unfavorable marker).
Patients with complex_karyotype=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with complex_karyotype=0.
Patients with flt3_itd=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with flt3_itd=0.
Patients with idh2_mutation=1 have a higher objective_response rate than patients with idh2_mutation=0.
Patients with secondary_aml=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with secondary_aml=0.
Patients with unfit_for_intensive=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with unfit_for_intensive=0.

analyses.result_summary: Response rate 22.13% in idh1_mutation+ vs 16.50% in wild-type; absolute difference +5.63 percentage points; chi-square p=3.5e-18. Strongly supported.
Response 17.32% in npm1_mutation+ vs 16.73% in wild-type; +0.59 pp; chi-square p=0.109. Direction matches but not significant.
Response 16.57% in tp53_mutation+ vs 16.94% in wild-type; -0.37 pp; chi-square p=0.52. Direction matches but null.
Response 16.81% in complex_karyotype+ vs 16.92% in normal; -0.12 pp; p=0.82. Null.
Response 17.19% in flt3_itd+ vs 16.83% in wild-type; +0.36 pp (opposite of hypothesized direction); p=0.40. Null.
Response 16.54% in idh2_mutation+ vs 16.94% in wild-type; -0.40 pp (opposite of hypothesized direction); p=0.50. Null.
Response 16.87% vs 16.92%; near-zero difference; p=0.92. Null.
Response 16.95% in unfit vs 16.87% in fit; near-zero; p=0.84. Null.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine=1 is associated with higher objective_response than treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine=0.
treatment_7plus3=1 is associated with higher objective_response than treatment_7plus3=0.
treatment_midostaurin=1 is associated with higher objective_response than treatment_midostaurin=0.
treatment_gilteritinib=1 is associated with higher objective_response than treatment_gilteritinib=0.
treatment_ivosidenib=1 is associated with higher objective_response than treatment_ivosidenib=0.
treatment_enasidenib=1 is associated with higher objective_response than treatment_enasidenib=0.

analyses.result_summary: Univariable logistic: log-OR=+0.0525, OR=1.054, RD=+0.74 pp, p=0.031. Modestly supported.
log-OR=-0.0511, OR=0.950, RD=-0.71 pp (opposite direction), p=0.13. Not supported.
log-OR=-0.0623, OR=0.940, RD=-0.86 pp (opposite direction), p=0.17. Not supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients with flt3_itd=1, treatment_midostaurin=1 produces a larger increase in objective_response than the same treatment among flt3_itd=0 patients (positive treatment_midostaurin x flt3_itd interaction on the log-odds scale).

Among patients with flt3_itd=1, treatment_gilteritinib=1 produces a larger increase in objective_response than the same treatment among flt3_itd=0 patients (positive treatment_gilteritinib x flt3_itd interaction).
Among patients with flt3_tkd=1, treatment_midostaurin=1 produces a larger increase in objective_response than among flt3_tkd=0 patients (positive interaction).
Among patients with flt3_tkd=1, treatment_gilteritinib=1 produces a larger increase in objective_response than among flt3_tkd=0 patients (positive interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction log-OR=-0.105, p=0.22. Subgroup RRs: flt3_itd+/mido+ 16.1% (n=1468) vs flt3_itd+/mido- 17.4%; flt3_itd-/mido+ 17.0% vs flt3_itd-/mido- 16.8%. Not supported (numerically opposite).
Interaction log-OR=+0.034, p=0.69. Subgroup RRs: flt3_itd+/gilt+ 16.9% (n=1491) vs flt3_itd+/gilt- 17.2%; no enrichment of benefit in FLT3-ITD+. Not supported.
Interaction log-OR=+0.023, p=0.87. Subgroup RRs flt3_tkd+/mido+ 16.5% vs flt3_tkd+/mido- 16.2%. Null.
Interaction log-OR=-0.152, p=0.33. flt3_tkd+/gilt+ 14.1% vs flt3_tkd+/gilt- 16.6% (numerically opposite). Not supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients with idh1_mutation=1, treatment_ivosidenib=1 produces a larger increase in objective_response than among idh1_mutation=0 patients (positive treatment_ivosidenib x idh1_mutation interaction). This is the canonical biomarker-matched indication.

Among patients with idh2_mutation=1, treatment_enasidenib=1 produces a larger increase in objective_response than among idh2_mutation=0 patients (positive treatment_enasidenib x idh2_mutation interaction). Canonical biomarker-matched indication.
Off-target check: treatment_ivosidenib does NOT produce a larger increase in objective_response among idh2_mutation=1 vs idh2_mutation=0 patients (no interaction expected).
Off-target check: treatment_enasidenib does NOT produce a larger increase in objective_response among idh1_mutation=1 vs idh1_mutation=0 patients.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction log-OR=-0.364, p=0.027. Subgroup RRs: idh1+/ivo+ 17.4% (n=298) vs idh1+/ivo- 22.5%; idh1-/ivo+ 17.1% vs idh1-/ivo- 16.4%. Direction OPPOSITE the hypothesized canonical benefit: ivosidenib appears to reduce response in IDH1-mutant patients.
Interaction log-OR=-0.124, p=0.45. Subgroup RRs: idh2+/ena+ 14.4% (n=354) vs idh2+/ena- 16.7%; numerically opposite the hypothesized direction; not significant.
Interaction log-OR=+0.062, p=0.69. No off-target benefit, as expected.
Interaction log-OR=+0.149, p=0.33. Subgroup RRs idh1+/ena+ 23.3% vs idh1+/ena- 22.0%; numerically positive but not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients with unfit_for_intensive=1, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine=1 yields a larger increase in objective_response than among unfit_for_intensive=0 patients (positive ven/aza x unfit interaction; ven/aza is the standard for unfit patients).

Among patients with unfit_for_intensive=1, treatment_7plus3=1 yields a SMALLER increase (or harm) in objective_response than among fit patients (negative 7+3 x unfit interaction; intensive chemo is poorly tolerated in unfit patients).
The benefit of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine on objective_response is larger in older patients (age>=75) than in patients <60.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction log-OR=+0.104, p=0.036. Subgroup RRs: unfit/ven+ 17.9% vs unfit/ven- 16.3% (RD +1.6 pp); fit/ven+ 17.0% vs fit/ven- 16.8% (RD +0.2 pp). Supported in direction and significance.
Interaction log-OR=-0.033, p=0.50. Direction matches the hypothesis (7+3 marginally worse in unfit) but not statistically significant.

RR difference (ven/aza+ minus ven/aza-): age<60 +0.4 pp, age 60-74 +1.2 pp, age>=75 +0.2 pp. No clean monotone gradient with age; benefit appears concentrated in middle-age stratum, not the oldest. Hypothesis (largest benefit in >=75) NOT supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with lower objective_response (negative coefficient on ecog_ps in adjusted logistic regression).
Older age_years is associated with lower objective_response, after adjusting for ECOG and disease biology.
After adjusting for age, ECOG, secondary AML, TP53, and complex karyotype, npm1_mutation remains a positive predictor of objective_response.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ age_years + ecog_ps + secondary_aml + tp53_mutation + complex_karyotype + npm1_mutation', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: ECOG coefficient -0.384 per unit (OR 0.68), p=4.9e-97 (Wald z=-20.96). Strongly supported.
Age coefficient +0.0004 per year (essentially zero), p=0.73. NOT supported: once ECOG and disease biology are accounted for, age has no independent association with response.
NPM1 coefficient +0.043, p=0.10 (z=1.64). Direction matches but not statistically significant after adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Self-identified Black patients have a lower objective_response rate than white patients.
Hispanic patients have a lower objective_response rate than white patients.
Asian patients have a different objective_response rate than white patients.
Uninsured patients have a lower objective_response rate than privately insured patients.
Patients with rural_residence=1 have a lower objective_response rate than urban patients.

analyses.result_summary: Black vs white log-OR=-0.112, p=0.004; raw response rates 15.74% (Black) vs 17.28% (white), absolute difference -1.54 pp. Supported.
Hispanic vs white log-OR=-0.042, p=0.22; raw RRs 16.70% vs 17.28%, -0.58 pp. Direction matches but not significant.
Asian vs white log-OR=-0.068, p=0.19; raw 16.33% vs 17.28%, -0.95 pp. Numerically lower but not significant.
Uninsured vs private log-OR=-0.003, p=0.97; medicaid +0.016 (p=0.68); medicare +0.037 (p=0.16). No insurance-related disparity. Not supported.
log-OR=+0.0002, p=0.99. RR rural 16.91% vs urban 16.90%. Null.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response (positive coefficient).
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher blast_pct_marrow is associated with lower objective_response.
Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio (nlr) is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher wbc_k_per_ul (presenting WBC) is associated with lower objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Univariable log-OR=+0.115 per g/dL, p=1.4e-6. In adjusted multivariable model coefficient remains +0.116 (p<0.001). Supported.
log-OR=-0.0070 per mg/L, p=2.8e-6. Persists in adjusted model (-0.0071, p<0.001). Supported.
log-OR=-0.0057 per percentage point of marrow blasts, p=1.2e-19. Strongly supported and persists in adjusted model.
log-OR=-0.0401 per percent weight loss, p=7.4e-35. Strongest single lab-style predictor. Stratified RRs: <=0% wl 18.6%, 0-5% 17.9%, 5-10% 15.1%, >10% 11.9%. Strongly supported.
log-OR=-0.00014 per U/L, p=0.35. Direction matches but null.
log-OR=-0.0022 per K/uL, p=1.9e-5. Direction matches and modestly significant. Supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: At least one of the 24 candidate SNPs (snp_rs*) shows an association with objective_response that survives Bonferroni correction (p<0.05/24=0.00208).
snp_rs1050828 is positively associated with objective_response (the most significant single SNP in screening).

analyses.code: for s in snps: smf.logit(f'objective_response ~ {s}', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Of 24 SNPs, 2 reached nominal p<0.05 (rs1050828 p=0.0053; rs2032582 p=0.049). Zero survived Bonferroni (p<0.0021). Hypothesis NOT supported; SNP panel as a whole shows no signal beyond chance.
Per-allele log-OR=+0.118, p=0.0053. Nominally significant and direction matches, but does not survive multiple-testing correction across the 24-SNP panel.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable logistic model that simultaneously adjusts for ECOG, age, key labs (albumin, CRP, blasts, weight loss), AML mutations, treatments, and race, ecog_ps remains the dominant negative predictor of objective_response.
In the same multivariable model, idh1_mutation remains a strong positive predictor of objective_response, independent of treatment and demographic factors.
After multivariable adjustment, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine retains a positive coefficient on objective_response.

After multivariable adjustment for clinical and biological covariates, Black race retains a negative association with objective_response (i.e., the disparity is not fully explained by measured covariates).
The benefit of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine on objective_response is larger in patients with ECOG 0 (fitter) than in those with ECOG 2 (sicker), in absolute response-rate terms.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ ecog_ps + age_years + albumin_g_dl + crp_mg_l + blast_pct_marrow + weight_loss_pct_6mo + idh1_mutation + npm1_mutation + tp53_mutation + complex_karyotype + flt3_itd + treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine + treatment_7plus3 + treatment_midostaurin + treatment_gilteritinib + treatment_ivosidenib + treatment_enasidenib + C(race_ethnicity, Treatment(\'white\'))', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable model: ECOG -0.383 (p<1e-95); IDH1 +0.362 (p<1e-16); ven/aza +0.058 (p=0.017); Black race -0.107 (p=0.006); albumin +0.117 (p<1e-5); CRP -0.0072 (p<1e-5); blasts -0.0057 (p<1e-18); weight loss -0.040 (p<1e-33). All four hypotheses (h10_mvr_ecog, h10_mvr_idh1, h10_mvr_venaza, h10_mvr_black) are supported in direction and statistical significance.
IDH1 mutation coefficient +0.362 (OR 1.44), p=1.3e-17 in the full multivariable model. Confirms IDH1 main effect is independent of treatment and other covariates.
ven/aza coefficient +0.058 (OR 1.06), p=0.017. Modest positive association persists after adjustment.

Black race coefficient -0.107 (OR 0.90), p=0.006 after adjustment for ECOG, labs, mutations, and treatment. The Black-vs-white disparity is essentially unchanged from the unadjusted estimate (-0.112), arguing against confounding by measured prognostic factors.

Absolute RR difference (ven/aza+ minus ven/aza-): ECOG 0 +1.43 pp (RR 22.1% vs 20.7%), ECOG 1 +0.34 pp, ECOG 2 +0.67 pp. Largest absolute benefit in ECOG 0 stratum, supporting the hypothesis that response benefit concentrates in fitter patients (though baseline response is also higher there).
